creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to John Marino from comment #4)
> er, um.
> 
> acknowledging this is a 10-second scan:
> 
> 1) "COMMENT= An anarchic control version system without unnecessary
> re-merges"
> portlint doesn't complain about that?  Did you run portlint?
> 
> 2) i don't understand hardcoding WITHOUT_PSYCO=yes
> Why not:
>   A) remove this line
>   B) remove pre-everything target (warning makes no sense if psyco is not an
> option)
>   C) remove the .ifndef(WITHOUT_PSYCO) entire section
> 
> Are you trying to preserve psycho functionality?  chances are this port will
> be removed (it should have already been removed IMO)
> 
> and WITH_* knobs are basically frowned upon nowadays
> 
> I thought you were going to remove all traces of psyco, what is it that you
> want to do with it?

Ugh.

Yes. I'm afraid this was a bit of a quickie. I have a meeting I need to attend shortly. I was hoping to get [this] back in the tree. So that I could manage the whole thing through svn(1). As I am /far/ more accustomed to that route.

Sorry. If you prefer, I'll take the time to re-do the whole mess, and post another (correct) shar(1) file here.

--Chris
